description: A quiet moment in the gym during midsummer heat. Adachi ties Shimamura's hair for the first time—a small, intimate act that reveals their delicate distance.
content:
Maybe it's because of my hair, I said as I pinched my bangs, feeling Adachi's eyes on me.

We were off in one corner of the gym, still drowning in the thick heat of midsummer.

What do you mean? she asked.

I mean it's hot. My hair's too long, so it traps the heat.

Oh…

I swept the long bundle resting on my shoulder to the side. Adachi nodded like she understood, then stared at the nearly empty plastic bottle in her hand. After a moment, she started shaking it rhythmically so the water sloshed loudly.

Feel any cooler yet?

Thanks for the effort.

The water inside probably wasn't cold anymore, but the sound still felt refreshing.

I closed my eyes, trying to sink into the illusion of coolness she was creating. It was almost like I could hear splashing behind my ears. Sweat trickled slowly down my skin. I tried to picture the sea, but since I didn't have any emotional attachment to oceans, all I could see was a murky void. Maybe a river would be easier.

That thought led me to my grandparents' house—and the old dog that lived there.

Which only made me feel hotter.

The sloshing suddenly stopped. I opened my eyes just as Adachi lifted the bottle to her lips and drank the rest.

Huh? You're done already?

My hand got tired.

No stamina at all. You'll never survive like that.

Probably not, she said with the tiniest smile.

Seeing that made me smile too. Well, she wasn't wrong. That's exactly why we skipped class and hid ourselves away in this cramped space, like two cats avoiding everyone's eyes.

The longer I sat still, the more my sweat-soaked hair clung to my neck, which was annoying. I needed to do something about it.

Can you tie my hair?

I pulled a hair tie from my bag and handed it to her. Adachi looked up from where she'd been rolling the empty bottle back and forth on the floor. Her gaze flicked between the hair tie and me.

Me?

Is there anyone else here?

If someone else was here, that'd be a problem—this was our hideout. Our shared refuge where the lazy, consequence-free air drifted with us. If someone else came in, that atmosphere would thin out and I'd probably suffocate.

Adachi took the hair tie and looped it around her fingers, twisting it absently.

Wait. I'll look it up.

She grabbed her phone and started searching. I leaned a bit closer to peek. The screen was full of hairstyle tutorials. So she was truly studying how to tie hair.

She didn't need to take it that seriously… she could've just tied it however. But if she was putting in this much effort out of kindness, I might as well accept it gracefully.

I stretched my legs out and waited while she learned. I wondered—maybe Adachi had never touched someone else's hair before. She'd told me she had no siblings, and her relationship with her mom didn't sound close. And she didn't look like someone with many friends. She probably never had the chance.

So… this is her first time?

I twirled a strand of my own hair.

Even if hair is soft and easy to handle, it's still part of your body. Entrusting it to someone probably requires a certain level of trust.

I believe in your taste, Adachi.

…Don't, she muttered.

After a while, I turned my back toward her, letting my hair fall into her hands.

Shimamura, your hair's dyed, right? she asked as she gathered it.

Yep. My entire family unanimously agrees it looks terrible.

The only person who ever complimented it was the stylist who dyed it—and I'm sure they'd praise me even if I walked out with neon pink hair. I could tell from the mirror they never once looked at me properly. Then again, when you stare at people's heads all day, I guess detachment is a job hazard.

Adachi's fingertips moved my hair left and right. Occasionally she brushed my scalp, sending tiny shivers down my back.

Honestly, I wasn't used to letting anyone besides family touch my head. And Adachi wasn't exactly dexterous.

Our awkwardness somehow synced perfectly.

I closed my eyes and let her careful, keyboard-like tapping motions work.

All done, she said.

I opened my eyes, feeling the air around my neck more clearly.

Well, well… you've made me stunningly beautiful—

Mid-sentence, I froze.

Do you have a mirror?

It's hard to know what my hair looks like without one.

I turned around. Adachi slowly shook her head.

Hmm…

A few strands had come loose—maybe due to her inexperience, maybe on purpose.

Eh, whatever.

I did say I trusted her taste. Whether I looked or not didn't matter. Besides, only Adachi could see it right now.

As long as we were satisfied, that was enough.

And my neck felt cooler anyway.

Adachi silently stared at me—at the hairstyle she'd chosen—her expression as unreadable as ever.

We weren't close enough yet for me to ask what she thought. The distance between us was delicate, fragile.

At least… for now.

Then Adachi finally looked away. Her gaze fell to her hand—the one that had just been holding my hair.
